activities_projects.title: Character Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare the qualities of Gerrard and the Intruder
steps: Read the dialogues of both characters
Write three qualities of Gerrard
Write three qualities of the Intruder
Compare them in a short paragraph

activities_projects.title: Intruder’s Motivation
activities_projects.objective: Analyse why the intruder wants Gerrard’s identity
steps: Read the part where the intruder explains his plan
Write 4–5 sentences explaining his reasons

activities_projects.title: Irony in the Play
activities_projects.objective: Identify ironic expressions used by Gerrard
steps: Find two sentences where Gerrard speaks ironically
Write what he actually means

activities_projects.title: Dialogue Analysis
steps: Choose one dialogue from the play
Explain what it shows about Gerrard or the Intruder
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands role of dialogue in drama

activities_projects.title: Plot Summary
activities_projects.objective: Summarise the main events of the play
steps: Identify the beginning, middle and ending
Write a short summary of the play in 5–6 sentences

activities_projects.title: Gerrard’s Intelligence
activities_projects.objective: Explain how Gerrard uses intelligence to escape
steps: Read the final scene carefully
Write 4–5 sentences describing Gerrard’s clever plan

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification
activities_projects.objective: Understand the main theme of the play
steps: Think about the lesson taught in the play
Write the theme in 3–4 sentences

activities_projects.title: Character Dialogue Writing
steps: Imagine a short conversation between Gerrard and a police officer
Write 5–6 lines of dialogue

activities_projects.title: Conflict in the Play
steps: Identify the problem faced by Gerrard
Explain how the conflict is resolved

activities_projects.title: Role Play Activity
steps: Choose a short scene from the play
Perform it with a partner in class
activities_projects.evaluation: Clear dialogue delivery and expression (10 marks)
